jokes.text: Yesterday Jaden Smith came in, and he was like, 'Look, I want to quit the music/acting business and work here with you.' And I was like, 'Jaden, be serious. The world needs you. You have a gift.'
jokes.setup: Tom talking about his business success
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim that Jaden Smith wants to work at his rental clothing store and Tom's self-important response
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific humor that perfectly captures Tom's delusional self-importance and celebrity obsession. The absurdity is grounded in character.

jokes.text: something he referred to as 'sympathy'?
jokes.setup: Lawyer explaining the client's offer to buy Tom's business
jokes.punchline: The lawyer's confusion about the concept of sympathy
jokes.explanation: Efficient character beat showing the lawyer's cold nature through a single word choice.

jokes.text: Get a big old mug of hot chocolate, put on my thinking PJs, and get back to you.
jokes.setup: Tom being asked to make a serious business decision
jokes.punchline: His childish decision-making process involving thinking PJs
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Tom's manchild personality with specific, quotable details.

jokes.text: We would also be interested in acquiring your thinking PJs.
jokes.setup: Tom's mention of thinking PJs
jokes.punchline: The lawyer's immediate deadpan interest in buying them too
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that escalates the absurdity while maintaining the lawyer's character.

jokes.text: No matter what happens, you will never acquire my thinking PJs or my YouTube blazer... Nonnegotiable.
jokes.setup: The lawyer wanting to buy his thinking PJs
jokes.punchline: Tom's dramatic declaration about protecting his ridiculous wardrobe items
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the addition of 'YouTube blazer' adding another absurd Tom-specific item.

jokes.text: Larry, everyone else was able to make it to the committee meeting on time. What's your excuse? / I was voting for you in the recall election.
jokes.setup: Leslie criticizing Larry for being late
jokes.punchline: Larry was actually doing something supportive for Leslie
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry dynamic where his good intentions backfire into criticism.

jokes.text: This says that I'm in charge of the silly skeleton room, when really it should say 'the undead serial killer room.' Who do I talk to about changing this?
jokes.setup: April reading her Halloween assignment
jokes.punchline: Her serious complaint about not getting a dark enough theme
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - taking a bureaucratic approach to getting more darkness.

jokes.text: Plus, um... It's Kevin Pollack's birthday. That's something to focus on today. Who cares about the recall? It's Kevin's day.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming she's not worried about the recall
jokes.punchline: Her desperate attempt to focus on a random celebrity birthday instead
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's nervous energy and tendency to over-compensate with random enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Ron, can you put some more tiny marshmallow in my hot chocky?
jokes.setup: Tom dealing with his business crisis
jokes.punchline: His childish language and dependence on Ron for comfort
jokes.explanation: Tom reverting to childlike behavior under stress, with the visual of him drinking hot chocolate.

jokes.text: Maybe you should try taking a walk... Out of my office.
jokes.setup: Ron appearing to offer helpful advice to Tom
jokes.punchline: Actually just telling him to leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - appearing helpful while actually being dismissive, with excellent structure.

jokes.text: Ron's in Bloosh! / What? / Ron's in Bloosh? / Ron is in Bloosh! / What is Bloosh?
jokes.setup: Tom discovering Ron is featured in a lifestyle magazine
jokes.punchline: The escalating excitement contrasted with Ron's confusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the contrast between Tom's world and Ron's complete disconnect from it.

jokes.text: She spent four months living in Kate Bosworth's pool house.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining Annabel Porter's credentials
jokes.punchline: This bizarre living arrangement being presented as impressive
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity culture reference that Tom would genuinely find impressive.

jokes.text: Annabel says that buying an island is the only real way to know that your goji-berry farm is pesticide-free.
jokes.setup: Tom reading from the lifestyle magazine
jokes.punchline: The completely over-the-top solution to a first-world problem
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of lifestyle guru absurdity with escalating ridiculousness.

jokes.text: Someone's getting a new leather jacket. / I don't want a leather jacket. / It's for me.
jokes.setup: Tom appearing to celebrate Ron's success
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually about Tom getting something for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom selfishness revealed through misdirection, very economical structure.

jokes.text: April, you seem depressed, and I would know. I spent most of last year being treated by Dr. Richard Nygard for my own emotional problems.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to help April with her sadness
jokes.punchline: His immediate oversharing about his own therapy
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris oversharing presented as helpful qualification for giving advice.

jokes.text: Every year, we would dress up like demons and egg Larry's house. / That was you? / Larry, please. We're having a private conversation.
jokes.setup: April explaining her Halloween tradition with Andy
jokes.punchline: Larry's recognition followed by April's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Great April dismissiveness of Larry combined with casual admission of property damage.

jokes.text: Nobody wants Gengurch-family-themed Jack-o-lanterns. / Gayle likes them.
jokes.setup: Leslie criticizing Larry's pumpkin carving
jokes.punchline: Larry's pathetic defense referencing his wife
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry incompetence with his typical weak defense pattern.

jokes.text: Why didn't we call it 'City Hall-oween'?
jokes.setup: Leslie realizing problems with their Halloween event
jokes.punchline: Her typical over-the-top pun solution
jokes.explanation: Very Leslie moment - solving problems with enthusiastic but cheesy wordplay.

jokes.text: A poster announcing the new Lilith Fair concert. It's fake, but it'll buy us an hour.
jokes.setup: Ben listing items to cheer up Leslie
jokes.punchline: Admitting they created fake news to temporarily distract her
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation showing how far they'll go to manage Leslie's emotions, very efficient.

jokes.text: We even have a secret hand signal in case we think she needs help. It's loosely based on the Klingon greeting salute. I just learned that. And if I had known, I would not have agreed to it.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining their support system for Leslie
jokes.punchline: His regret about the nerdy hand signal choice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's self-awareness about his nerd tendencies.

jokes.text: Your brain is almost as perfect as your face.
jokes.setup: Leslie complimenting Ann's idea
jokes.punchline: The backhanded way of saying Ann's face is more perfect than her brain
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie compliment that accidentally insults while trying to be nice.

jokes.text: Hey, Ron, baby, what are the hot deets on Bloosh? / Start over and speak differently.
jokes.setup: Tom using his typical millennial slang with Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's blunt rejection of Tom's entire communication style
jokes.explanation: Perfect clash of characters with Ron's economical shutdown of Tom's verbal style. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Annabel, could I be more jels of your low-lights right now? I mean, O.M.Squee, talk about bangs envy.
jokes.setup: Tom and Donna teaching Ron lifestyle magazine speak
jokes.punchline: The completely ridiculous language they want Ron to use
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of lifestyle magazine language with invented slang that's both absurd and believable.

jokes.text: Oof, you got a long way to go, Swanson. / I regret everything.
jokes.setup: Ron attempting to practice the lifestyle magazine speak
jokes.punchline: Ron's immediate regret about agreeing to help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction to being forced outside his comfort zone, very economical.

jokes.text: Too close to call, a real nail-biter, anybody's game... These are three phrases you won't hear tonight, as Leslie Knope has been voted out of office.
jokes.setup: Setting up expectation of a close race
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was actually a landslide defeat
jokes.explanation: Clever structure that subverts expectations while delivering bad news in a darkly comic way.

jokes.text: ♪ I got-a the pizza, pizza time, it's-a pizza time! / Larry, everyone is miserable, and you are only making it worse.
jokes.setup: Larry arriving with pizzas during the devastating election results
jokes.punchline: His oblivious cheerful song contrasted with everyone's misery
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with Larry's terrible timing and Ben's blunt assessment.

jokes.text: I left her, like, 30 voice mails, and her outgoing message is just her sobbing and burping and crazy laughing.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining she's been trying to reach Leslie
jokes.punchline: The specific description of Leslie's breakdown voicemail
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific, visceral details that paint a vivid picture of Leslie's state.

jokes.text: What's up, fartwads?
jokes.setup: Leslie entering after her devastating loss
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriately casual and crude greeting
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's uncharacteristic coping mechanism through completely un-Leslie language.

jokes.text: This is really good. Why did I ever fight this?
jokes.setup: Leslie eating a Paunch Burger after always fighting them
jokes.punchline: Her defeated acceptance of what she once opposed
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal showing Leslie's defeated state through actions contradicting her values.

jokes.text: Eat my shorts, Jabronies. Knope, out.
jokes.setup: Leslie being asked about her concession speech
jokes.punchline: Her completely inappropriate and brief 'speech'
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast to Leslie's usual verbose, diplomatic nature. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: And you know what I think it needs, actually, is a papier-mâché li'l Sebastian!
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to focus on work projects
jokes.punchline: Her manic suggestion involving the beloved dead horse
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series mythology showing Leslie's manic state through inappropriate suggestions.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go fall asleep on a bench.
jokes.setup: Leslie's manic behavior reaching a breaking point
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact declaration of giving up completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of complete defeat, very economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: The average woman worries about what she looks like in a bathing suit. So does my nanny. So I get it.
jokes.setup: Annabel trying to relate to average women
jokes.punchline: Revealing her disconnect by comparing to her nanny instead of herself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing wealth privilege through attempted empathy that misses the mark.

jokes.text: First of all, Mozambique cashmere is the new cast-iron stove.
jokes.setup: Annabel being asked about trending items
jokes.punchline: The completely nonsensical comparison between luxury fabric and cookware
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of lifestyle guru nonsense with absurd trend comparisons.

jokes.text: my favorite fishmonger now makes house calls. / Thank God. / Finally.
jokes.setup: Annabel sharing lifestyle updates
jokes.punchline: Joan's enthusiastic agreement about a ridiculously privileged service
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of privilege absurdity with Joan's enthusiastic enabling.

jokes.text: Did that woman call my chair 'delicious'?
jokes.setup: Annabel describing Ron's chairs in lifestyle terms
jokes.punchline: Ron's confusion at the inappropriate food adjective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction to lifestyle magazine language applied to his practical woodwork.

jokes.text: Scary stuff is invisible, Leah... Broken dreams, disappointment, achieving your greatest goal and having it all fall apart and knowing that you'll never climb any higher.
jokes.setup: Kids asking where the scary stuff is in the haunted house
jokes.punchline: Leslie explaining existential dread to children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy mixing Leslie's depression with inappropriate child interaction. High craft for earned darkness.

jokes.text: I'm gonna give you something sweeter than candy, Ryan... The truth. It's very possible that some of you have already peaked. It's all downhill from here, turkeys.
jokes.setup: Kids asking for candy
jokes.punchline: Leslie offering existential despair instead
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy escalation. The phrase 'sweeter than candy... the truth' is brilliantly constructed.

jokes.text: Just remember, kids, nothing gold can stay.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying goodbye to trick-or-treaters
jokes.punchline: Ending with a depressing Robert Frost quote
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the dark children's interaction with literary sophistication.

jokes.text: Yes, mostly you use it for sitting.
jokes.setup: Annabel describing multiple uses for Ron's chairs
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact statement about chairs' primary function
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - cutting through lifestyle nonsense with practical truth. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: We don't do meals in my home. No, every two hours, we eat what I like to call a food tease, like an oat wedge or a seaweed lozenge.
jokes.setup: Ron suggesting using chairs for eating meals
jokes.punchline: Annabel's completely absurd eating philosophy and terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of wellness culture with invented terms 'food tease' and specific absurd examples.

jokes.text: I love your hair. / Oh. Thank you. / It's genetic and unattainable.
jokes.setup: Annabel complimenting Joan's hair
jokes.punchline: Joan's immediately self-absorbed response that's both humble and boastful
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Joan's narcissism disguised as humility.

jokes.text: Bert Macklin is not a lifeguard. He's an FBI agent. / Really? That's even harder to believe.
jokes.setup: Chris trying to play Andy's character incorrectly
jokes.punchline: April's correction followed by Chris's logical assessment
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Chris's logical response to the absurd character concept.

jokes.text: Now I'm sad that I miss Andy and sad that I can't make fun of you to Andy.
jokes.setup: April explaining why Chris's help isn't working
jokes.punchline: Her additional layer of sadness about not being able to share mockery
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - missing both the person and the opportunity to mock others with them.

jokes.text: I've failed you, both as a scary monster and a friend.
jokes.setup: Chris realizing his attempts to help April aren't working
jokes.punchline: His sincere self-assessment of dual failure
jokes.explanation: Good Chris moment showing his earnest approach to even silly situations.

jokes.text: I was impeached when I was 18, and it was brutal.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to relate to Leslie's political defeat
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to his own teenage political scandal
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ben's backstory used for character empathy moment.

jokes.text: Oh, God, did I peak when I was 18?
jokes.setup: Ben and Leslie discussing accepting that you've peaked
jokes.punchline: Ben's horrifying realization about his own life trajectory
jokes.explanation: Great character moment where Ben realizes his attempt to help backfired into personal crisis.

jokes.text: B-13 shots. Bird bath salts. Wha...? Champagne decanters!
jokes.setup: Tom at the upscale party discovering luxury items
jokes.punchline: His increasingly excited reactions to fancy party accessories
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tom's materialistic excitement at luxury items.

jokes.text: Well, I make two a year, so maybe in six years.
jokes.setup: Woman wanting to buy 12 of Ron's chairs
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact math about his slow production rate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment showing his commitment to craftsmanship over commerce.

jokes.text: Yes, and now they will be made by thousands of tiny Chinese hands.
jokes.setup: Ron saying his chairs are handmade
jokes.punchline: Annabel's casual acknowledgment of exploitative manufacturing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about manufacturing that shows Annabel's disconnect from ethical concerns.

jokes.text: He's a big fan of your... Whatever you call what you do.
jokes.setup: Ron introducing Tom to Annabel
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to describe or validate lifestyle guru work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron dismissiveness disguised as an introduction. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, no, I am not done with you, you wicked little so-and-so.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to leave after introducing Tom
jokes.punchline: Annabel's overly dramatic pursuit using old-fashioned language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annabel's dramatic flair and persistence.

jokes.text: Hello, sir. We are sober. We would like to get tattoos on our bodies, 'cause we love each other.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben entering a tattoo parlor
jokes.punchline: Leslie's overly formal and questionably accurate declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect drunk Leslie formality applied to an informal situation. Highly quotable structure.

jokes.text: Tattoos? This is a pawnshop. But, yeah, sure, I can do that.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for tattoos
jokes.punchline: Clarifying it's the wrong business but agreeing anyway
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with efficient structure showing the sketchy nature of the situation.

jokes.text: I want a portrait of Eleanor Roosevelt, tastefully done, and then she has a very classy tattoo of Pat Benatar on her arm. And that's very subtle.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her tattoo idea
jokes.punchline: The completely absurd and contradictory concept she considers subtle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity perfectly captured in Leslie's idea of 'subtle.' Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: Okay, you said you wanted 'mouth queen'?
jokes.setup: The tattoo artist trying to confirm Ben's tattoo
jokes.punchline: The completely wrong and inappropriate interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the miscommunication in the sketchy tattoo situation.

jokes.text: Good-bye, world traveler. Later, bro Heisen
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben leaving the pawnshop
jokes.punchline: Their nonsensical drunk farewells to the pawnshop owner
jokes.explanation: Good drunk character moment with their random, meaningless farewell names

jokes.text: My gravestone's gonna be a 60-inch touchscreen with a hologram of four mes singing End of the Road by Boyz II Men... But point taken.
jokes.setup: Ron's philosophical point about gravestones and names
jokes.punchline: Tom's typically over-the-top and specific technological fantasy
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Tom moment with specific absurd details and perfect character contrast to Ron's simplicity.

jokes.text: Don't let her get a tattoo. / What the hell happened? / I couldn't help it. She's so cute when she's coming up with destructive ideas.
jokes.setup: Ben failing at his assigned task in the support group
jokes.punchline: His romantic justification for enabling Leslie's bad decisions
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Ben's weakness for Leslie even in crisis situations.

jokes.text: Never send a husband to do a best friend's job.
jokes.setup: Ben failing to stop Leslie from getting a tattoo
jokes.punchline: Ann's assessment of the different relationship dynamics
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor about relationship roles, very quotable structure.

jokes.text: Ann, you poetic and noble land mermaid, you're right once again.
jokes.setup: Leslie being grateful to Ann for helping her refocus
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically over-the-top compliment using absurd imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie elaborate compliment with memorable specific imagery. Part of running gag.

jokes.text: I flushed my car keys down the toilet.
jokes.setup: Leslie appearing to be back on track
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of another drunk decision
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Leslie's night isn't quite over, economical delivery.

jokes.text: But the truth is, I'm very much in love with Ann. / Okay, gross. The spell's broken.
jokes.setup: April seeming to warm up to Chris
jokes.punchline: Her immediate recoil when he gets sincere about Ann
jokes.explanation: Perfect April reaction to Chris's earnestness, good character consistency.

jokes.text: Ron said there's nothing more valuable than my name, but he's not a businessman. I am.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his decision to sell his business
jokes.punchline: His misplaced confidence in his business skills despite current failure
jokes.explanation: Good character irony showing Tom's delusional self-assessment as a businessman.

jokes.text: Ann, it was very hard to capture your beauty, and, Ben, you make a sexy pumpkin... No surprise.
jokes.setup: Leslie presenting carved pumpkins of her friends
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically over-the-top compliments about the pumpkin versions
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her return to characteristic enthusiasm and compliment style.
